神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG {{commons category|Category:Kandani-jinja}} [[EN:Kandani Shrine]] [[JA:神谷神社]] [[SV:Kandani-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q11590885}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Kandani Shrine in Kagawa Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]
Honden (National Treasure) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|19|29.47|N|133|55|00.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kandani Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}}) |本殿 = Three-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Kandani Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:神谷神社 正面鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Front torii]] '''Kandani Shrine''' (かんだにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === It stands on the slope of Shiramine-yama, one of the peaks of {{ill|lt=Goshikidai|Q11372616|WD=Q11372616|1=Goshikidai}}, in the eastern part of Sakaide. It is located in a valley called "Kandani," with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kandani River|1=UNKNOWN}} flowing north of the precinct. Its honden is the oldest known {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} shrine building with a clearly known construction year[http://www.shikoku.gr.jp/spot/380 Kandani Shrine], and is designated a National Treasure. Although it possesses a National Treasure shrine building, the precinct itself is relatively small. It also preserves shrine treasures including nationally designated Important Cultural Property standing attendant statues. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deities''' * '''{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}''' (ほむすびのみこと) * '''Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひこのみこと) * '''Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-kami|1=Futsunushi}} (ふつぬしのかみ) ** {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} (たけみかづちのかみ) ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (あめのこやねのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=Hime Ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} (ひめおおかみ) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the valley where the shrine stands has long been called "Kandani." Behind the shrine is a giant rock called "Eikō-seki," identified as an ancient {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred rock, and Yayoi pottery has been found nearby. Regarding its founding, tradition states that in {{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}} ({{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3), Atto Ōtaru, uncle of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, built shrine buildings and invited the Kasuga Four Deities into the co-enshrined hall. In {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7), it was granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper; in {{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}} (Jogan 10), Senior Fifth Rank Lower. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}} ({{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5), it appears in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} as "Kandani Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. The ridge beam of the current honden bears an ink inscription from {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} ({{ill|lt=Kenpo|Q1194251|WD=Q1194251|1=Kempo (era)}} 7): "Treasure hall of Senior First Rank Kandani Daimyojin... begun on 10th day, 2nd month, year of tsuchinoto-u... by manor official Gyōbu no Sukune Masanaga," showing it had reached the highest divine rank and regional reverence at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system. On New Year's Eve of {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110|1=1927}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 2), a lamp fire ignited the haiden, causing a fire. The haiden burned down, but residents pulled the collapsing building away from the honden side, saving it. The present haiden was rebuilt from {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} (Showa 3) to {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Showa 5). On {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|WD=Q2884|1=September 27}}, {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), lightning struck the honden and about one-third of the roof was burned{{Cite web|和書|title=Kagawa: roof of National Treasure honden burned / lightning strike at Kandani Shrine |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/national/social/20220927000464 |date=2022-09-27 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2022-09-27}}. On October 12, {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|WD=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), a ground-breaking ceremony for preservation and repair was held, with completion planned for autumn 2025{{Cite web|和書|title=First step toward restoration of National Treasure honden: ground-breaking at Kandani Shrine in Sakaide, roof repair planned to finish autumn 2025 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/dg/article.aspx?id=K2023101300000001400 |date=2023-10-13 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2023-10-15}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), October 9: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kandani deity". * Jogan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), May 27: Senior Fifth Rank Lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Kandani Tenjin". === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri shrine building rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} (Kenpo 7), early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, by manor official Gyōbu Masanaga from the old honden. It is the oldest known shrine architecture in Japan with a clear construction date and is designated a National Treasure. About 50 meters behind the shrine buildings stands a giant rock called "Eikō-seki." It is said that in ancient times worship was performed there as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred site. ファイル:神谷神社 拝所.JPG|Haisho ファイル:神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii ファイル:神谷神社 御旅所.JPG|Otabisho
Located beside the first torii. === Sessha and massha === === Sessha === [[ファイル:Kandani-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Aragami Shrine]] * Aragami Shrine - enshrined partway along the approach. === Massha === Many are enshrined within the precinct. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Honden - designated {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|WD=Q2313|1=February 2}}, {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} (Showa 30). === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Two wooden standing attendant statues *: A pair of A and Un figures, considered early Kamakura works. Designated {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}}, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|WD=Q2649|1=1966}} (Showa 41). === Sakaide City designated tangible cultural properties === * Jinja Hōraku Renga (calligraphy) *: A 100-verse {{ill|lt=renga|Q1506883|WD=Q1506883|1=Renga}} sequence performed before the shrine in {{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|WD=Q6687|1=1493}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2), written on four half-folded torinoko sheets. Designated Feb 21, 1958. * Two bugaku masks (craftworks) *: Two masks for Batō and Kangenraku. Designated Nov 3, 1961. * Hand-copied Daihannya Haramita Sutra, 600 volumes (documents) *: Inscribed {{ill|lt=1455|Q6480|WD=Q6480|1=1455}} ({{ill|lt=Kyotoku|Q1143370|WD=Q1143370|1=Kyōtoku}} 4) and {{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 13). Designated Jan 10, 1967. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Sakaide Station|Q873215|WD=Q873215|1=Sakaide Station}} ({{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Kotohira Bus|Q11573750|WD=Q11573750|1=Kotosan Bus}} (bound for Ogoshi), get off at "Takaya Minami" (about 15 minutes), then walk 20 minutes. === References === * ''Engishiki-listed National Treasure Kandani Shrine'' (shrine history booklet) * Kagawa Historical Walk Editorial Committee, ''New Edition: Historical Walks in Kagawa Prefecture'', Yamakawa Publishing, 1996, p.28 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]

Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}}) |本殿 = Three-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Kandani Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:神谷神社 正面鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Front torii]] '''Kandani Shrine''' (かんだにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === It stands on the slope of Shiramine-yama, one of the peaks of {{ill|lt=Goshikidai|Q11372616|WD=Q11372616|1=Goshikidai}}, in the eastern part of Sakaide. It is located in a valley called "Kandani," with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kandani River|1=UNKNOWN}} flowing north of the precinct. Its honden is the oldest known {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} shrine building with a clearly known construction year[http://www.shikoku.gr.jp/spot/380 Kandani Shrine], and is designated a National Treasure. Although it possesses a National Treasure shrine building, the precinct itself is relatively small. It also preserves shrine treasures including nationally designated Important Cultural Property standing attendant statues. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deities''' * '''{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}''' (ほむすびのみこと) * '''Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひこのみこと) * '''Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-kami|1=Futsunushi}} (ふつぬしのかみ) ** {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} (たけみかづちのかみ) ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (あめのこやねのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=Hime Ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} (ひめおおかみ) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the valley where the shrine stands has long been called "Kandani." Behind the shrine is a giant rock called "Eikō-seki," identified as an ancient {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred rock, and Yayoi pottery has been found nearby. Regarding its founding, tradition states that in {{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}} ({{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3), Atto Ōtaru, uncle of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, built shrine buildings and invited the Kasuga Four Deities into the co-enshrined hall. In {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7), it was granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper; in {{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}} (Jogan 10), Senior Fifth Rank Lower. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}} ({{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5), it appears in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} as "Kandani Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. The ridge beam of the current honden bears an ink inscription from {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} ({{ill|lt=Kenpo|Q1194251|WD=Q1194251|1=Kempo (era)}} 7): "Treasure hall of Senior First Rank Kandani Daimyojin... begun on 10th day, 2nd month, year of tsuchinoto-u... by manor official Gyōbu no Sukune Masanaga," showing it had reached the highest divine rank and regional reverence at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system. On New Year's Eve of {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110|1=1927}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 2), a lamp fire ignited the haiden, causing a fire. The haiden burned down, but residents pulled the collapsing building away from the honden side, saving it. The present haiden was rebuilt from {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} (Showa 3) to {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Showa 5). On {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|WD=Q2884|1=September 27}}, {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), lightning struck the honden and about one-third of the roof was burned{{Cite web|和書|title=Kagawa: roof of National Treasure honden burned / lightning strike at Kandani Shrine |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/national/social/20220927000464 |date=2022-09-27 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2022-09-27}}. On October 12, {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|WD=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), a ground-breaking ceremony for preservation and repair was held, with completion planned for autumn 2025{{Cite web|和書|title=First step toward restoration of National Treasure honden: ground-breaking at Kandani Shrine in Sakaide, roof repair planned to finish autumn 2025 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/dg/article.aspx?id=K2023101300000001400 |date=2023-10-13 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2023-10-15}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), October 9: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kandani deity". * Jogan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), May 27: Senior Fifth Rank Lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Kandani Tenjin". === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri shrine building rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} (Kenpo 7), early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, by manor official Gyōbu Masanaga from the old honden. It is the oldest known shrine architecture in Japan with a clear construction date and is designated a National Treasure. About 50 meters behind the shrine buildings stands a giant rock called "Eikō-seki." It is said that in ancient times worship was performed there as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred site. ファイル:神谷神社 拝所.JPG|Haisho ファイル:神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii ファイル:神谷神社 御旅所.JPG|Otabisho
Located beside the first torii. === Sessha and massha === === Sessha === [[ファイル:Kandani-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Aragami Shrine]] * Aragami Shrine - enshrined partway along the approach. === Massha === Many are enshrined within the precinct. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Honden - designated {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|WD=Q2313|1=February 2}}, {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} (Showa 30). === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Two wooden standing attendant statues *: A pair of A and Un figures, considered early Kamakura works. Designated {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}}, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|WD=Q2649|1=1966}} (Showa 41). === Sakaide City designated tangible cultural properties === * Jinja Hōraku Renga (calligraphy) *: A 100-verse {{ill|lt=renga|Q1506883|WD=Q1506883|1=Renga}} sequence performed before the shrine in {{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|WD=Q6687|1=1493}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2), written on four half-folded torinoko sheets. Designated Feb 21, 1958. * Two bugaku masks (craftworks) *: Two masks for Batō and Kangenraku. Designated Nov 3, 1961. * Hand-copied Daihannya Haramita Sutra, 600 volumes (documents) *: Inscribed {{ill|lt=1455|Q6480|WD=Q6480|1=1455}} ({{ill|lt=Kyotoku|Q1143370|WD=Q1143370|1=Kyōtoku}} 4) and {{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 13). Designated Jan 10, 1967. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Sakaide Station|Q873215|WD=Q873215|1=Sakaide Station}} ({{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Kotohira Bus|Q11573750|WD=Q11573750|1=Kotosan Bus}} (bound for Ogoshi), get off at "Takaya Minami" (about 15 minutes), then walk 20 minutes. === References === * ''Engishiki-listed National Treasure Kandani Shrine'' (shrine history booklet) * Kagawa Historical Walk Editorial Committee, ''New Edition: Historical Walks in Kagawa Prefecture'', Yamakawa Publishing, 1996, p.28 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kandani Shrine entry === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Kandani-jinja}} * 神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG {{commons category|Category:Kandani-jinja}} [[EN:Kandani Shrine]] [[JA:神谷神社]] [[SV:Kandani-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q11590885}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Kandani Shrine in Kagawa Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]
Honden (National Treasure) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|19|29.47|N|133|55|00.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kandani Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}}) |本殿 = Three-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Kandani Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:神谷神社 正面鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Front torii]] '''Kandani Shrine''' (かんだにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === It stands on the slope of Shiramine-yama, one of the peaks of {{ill|lt=Goshikidai|Q11372616|WD=Q11372616|1=Goshikidai}}, in the eastern part of Sakaide. It is located in a valley called "Kandani," with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kandani River|1=UNKNOWN}} flowing north of the precinct. Its honden is the oldest known {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} shrine building with a clearly known construction year[http://www.shikoku.gr.jp/spot/380 Kandani Shrine], and is designated a National Treasure. Although it possesses a National Treasure shrine building, the precinct itself is relatively small. It also preserves shrine treasures including nationally designated Important Cultural Property standing attendant statues. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deities''' * '''{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}''' (ほむすびのみこと) * '''Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひこのみこと) * '''Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-kami|1=Futsunushi}} (ふつぬしのかみ) ** {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} (たけみかづちのかみ) ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (あめのこやねのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=Hime Ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} (ひめおおかみ) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the valley where the shrine stands has long been called "Kandani." Behind the shrine is a giant rock called "Eikō-seki," identified as an ancient {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred rock, and Yayoi pottery has been found nearby. Regarding its founding, tradition states that in {{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}} ({{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3), Atto Ōtaru, uncle of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, built shrine buildings and invited the Kasuga Four Deities into the co-enshrined hall. In {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7), it was granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper; in {{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}} (Jogan 10), Senior Fifth Rank Lower. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}} ({{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5), it appears in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} as "Kandani Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. The ridge beam of the current honden bears an ink inscription from {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} ({{ill|lt=Kenpo|Q1194251|WD=Q1194251|1=Kempo (era)}} 7): "Treasure hall of Senior First Rank Kandani Daimyojin... begun on 10th day, 2nd month, year of tsuchinoto-u... by manor official Gyōbu no Sukune Masanaga," showing it had reached the highest divine rank and regional reverence at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system. On New Year's Eve of {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110|1=1927}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 2), a lamp fire ignited the haiden, causing a fire. The haiden burned down, but residents pulled the collapsing building away from the honden side, saving it. The present haiden was rebuilt from {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} (Showa 3) to {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Showa 5). On {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|WD=Q2884|1=September 27}}, {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), lightning struck the honden and about one-third of the roof was burned{{Cite web|和書|title=Kagawa: roof of National Treasure honden burned / lightning strike at Kandani Shrine |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/national/social/20220927000464 |date=2022-09-27 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2022-09-27}}. On October 12, {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|WD=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), a ground-breaking ceremony for preservation and repair was held, with completion planned for autumn 2025{{Cite web|和書|title=First step toward restoration of National Treasure honden: ground-breaking at Kandani Shrine in Sakaide, roof repair planned to finish autumn 2025 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/dg/article.aspx?id=K2023101300000001400 |date=2023-10-13 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2023-10-15}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), October 9: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kandani deity". * Jogan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), May 27: Senior Fifth Rank Lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Kandani Tenjin". === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri shrine building rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} (Kenpo 7), early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, by manor official Gyōbu Masanaga from the old honden. It is the oldest known shrine architecture in Japan with a clear construction date and is designated a National Treasure. About 50 meters behind the shrine buildings stands a giant rock called "Eikō-seki." It is said that in ancient times worship was performed there as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred site. ファイル:神谷神社 拝所.JPG|Haisho ファイル:神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii ファイル:神谷神社 御旅所.JPG|Otabisho
Located beside the first torii. === Sessha and massha === === Sessha === [[ファイル:Kandani-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Aragami Shrine]] * Aragami Shrine - enshrined partway along the approach. === Massha === Many are enshrined within the precinct. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Honden - designated {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|WD=Q2313|1=February 2}}, {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} (Showa 30). === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Two wooden standing attendant statues *: A pair of A and Un figures, considered early Kamakura works. Designated {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}}, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|WD=Q2649|1=1966}} (Showa 41). === Sakaide City designated tangible cultural properties === * Jinja Hōraku Renga (calligraphy) *: A 100-verse {{ill|lt=renga|Q1506883|WD=Q1506883|1=Renga}} sequence performed before the shrine in {{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|WD=Q6687|1=1493}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2), written on four half-folded torinoko sheets. Designated Feb 21, 1958. * Two bugaku masks (craftworks) *: Two masks for Batō and Kangenraku. Designated Nov 3, 1961. * Hand-copied Daihannya Haramita Sutra, 600 volumes (documents) *: Inscribed {{ill|lt=1455|Q6480|WD=Q6480|1=1455}} ({{ill|lt=Kyotoku|Q1143370|WD=Q1143370|1=Kyōtoku}} 4) and {{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 13). Designated Jan 10, 1967. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Sakaide Station|Q873215|WD=Q873215|1=Sakaide Station}} ({{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Kotohira Bus|Q11573750|WD=Q11573750|1=Kotosan Bus}} (bound for Ogoshi), get off at "Takaya Minami" (about 15 minutes), then walk 20 minutes. === References === * ''Engishiki-listed National Treasure Kandani Shrine'' (shrine history booklet) * Kagawa Historical Walk Editorial Committee, ''New Edition: Historical Walks in Kagawa Prefecture'', Yamakawa Publishing, 1996, p.28 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kandani Shrine entry === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Kandani-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkashinkou/kandanijinjya.html National Treasure Kandani Shrine Honden] (Sakaide City website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kandani Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kandani Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:香川県の国宝]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kagawa prefecture]] [[Category:香川県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]]

Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]

Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}}) |本殿 = Three-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Kandani Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:神谷神社 正面鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Front torii]] '''Kandani Shrine''' (かんだにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === It stands on the slope of Shiramine-yama, one of the peaks of {{ill|lt=Goshikidai|Q11372616|WD=Q11372616|1=Goshikidai}}, in the eastern part of Sakaide. It is located in a valley called "Kandani," with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kandani River|1=UNKNOWN}} flowing north of the precinct. Its honden is the oldest known {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} shrine building with a clearly known construction year[http://www.shikoku.gr.jp/spot/380 Kandani Shrine], and is designated a National Treasure. Although it possesses a National Treasure shrine building, the precinct itself is relatively small. It also preserves shrine treasures including nationally designated Important Cultural Property standing attendant statues. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deities''' * '''{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}''' (ほむすびのみこと) * '''Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひこのみこと) * '''Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-kami|1=Futsunushi}} (ふつぬしのかみ) ** {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} (たけみかづちのかみ) ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (あめのこやねのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=Hime Ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} (ひめおおかみ) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the valley where the shrine stands has long been called "Kandani." Behind the shrine is a giant rock called "Eikō-seki," identified as an ancient {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred rock, and Yayoi pottery has been found nearby. Regarding its founding, tradition states that in {{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}} ({{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3), Atto Ōtaru, uncle of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, built shrine buildings and invited the Kasuga Four Deities into the co-enshrined hall. In {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7), it was granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper; in {{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}} (Jogan 10), Senior Fifth Rank Lower. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}} ({{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5), it appears in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} as "Kandani Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. The ridge beam of the current honden bears an ink inscription from {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} ({{ill|lt=Kenpo|Q1194251|WD=Q1194251|1=Kempo (era)}} 7): "Treasure hall of Senior First Rank Kandani Daimyojin... begun on 10th day, 2nd month, year of tsuchinoto-u... by manor official Gyōbu no Sukune Masanaga," showing it had reached the highest divine rank and regional reverence at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system. On New Year's Eve of {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110|1=1927}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 2), a lamp fire ignited the haiden, causing a fire. The haiden burned down, but residents pulled the collapsing building away from the honden side, saving it. The present haiden was rebuilt from {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} (Showa 3) to {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Showa 5). On {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|WD=Q2884|1=September 27}}, {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), lightning struck the honden and about one-third of the roof was burned{{Cite web|和書|title=Kagawa: roof of National Treasure honden burned / lightning strike at Kandani Shrine |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/national/social/20220927000464 |date=2022-09-27 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2022-09-27}}. On October 12, {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|WD=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), a ground-breaking ceremony for preservation and repair was held, with completion planned for autumn 2025{{Cite web|和書|title=First step toward restoration of National Treasure honden: ground-breaking at Kandani Shrine in Sakaide, roof repair planned to finish autumn 2025 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/dg/article.aspx?id=K2023101300000001400 |date=2023-10-13 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2023-10-15}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), October 9: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kandani deity". * Jogan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), May 27: Senior Fifth Rank Lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Kandani Tenjin". === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri shrine building rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} (Kenpo 7), early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, by manor official Gyōbu Masanaga from the old honden. It is the oldest known shrine architecture in Japan with a clear construction date and is designated a National Treasure. About 50 meters behind the shrine buildings stands a giant rock called "Eikō-seki." It is said that in ancient times worship was performed there as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred site. ファイル:神谷神社 拝所.JPG|Haisho ファイル:神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii ファイル:神谷神社 御旅所.JPG|Otabisho
Located beside the first torii. === Sessha and massha === === Sessha === [[ファイル:Kandani-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Aragami Shrine]] * Aragami Shrine - enshrined partway along the approach. === Massha === Many are enshrined within the precinct. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Honden - designated {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|WD=Q2313|1=February 2}}, {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} (Showa 30). === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Two wooden standing attendant statues *: A pair of A and Un figures, considered early Kamakura works. Designated {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}}, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|WD=Q2649|1=1966}} (Showa 41). === Sakaide City designated tangible cultural properties === * Jinja Hōraku Renga (calligraphy) *: A 100-verse {{ill|lt=renga|Q1506883|WD=Q1506883|1=Renga}} sequence performed before the shrine in {{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|WD=Q6687|1=1493}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2), written on four half-folded torinoko sheets. Designated Feb 21, 1958. * Two bugaku masks (craftworks) *: Two masks for Batō and Kangenraku. Designated Nov 3, 1961. * Hand-copied Daihannya Haramita Sutra, 600 volumes (documents) *: Inscribed {{ill|lt=1455|Q6480|WD=Q6480|1=1455}} ({{ill|lt=Kyotoku|Q1143370|WD=Q1143370|1=Kyōtoku}} 4) and {{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 13). Designated Jan 10, 1967. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Sakaide Station|Q873215|WD=Q873215|1=Sakaide Station}} ({{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Kotohira Bus|Q11573750|WD=Q11573750|1=Kotosan Bus}} (bound for Ogoshi), get off at "Takaya Minami" (about 15 minutes), then walk 20 minutes. === References === * ''Engishiki-listed National Treasure Kandani Shrine'' (shrine history booklet) * Kagawa Historical Walk Editorial Committee, ''New Edition: Historical Walks in Kagawa Prefecture'', Yamakawa Publishing, 1996, p.28 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kandani Shrine entry === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Kandani-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkashinkou/kandanijinjya.html National Treasure Kandani Shrine Honden] (Sakaide City website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kandani Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kandani Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:香川県の国宝]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kagawa prefecture]] [[Category:香川県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakaide, Kagawa]]

神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG {{commons category|Category:Kandani-jinja}} [[EN:Kandani Shrine]] [[JA:神谷神社]] [[SV:Kandani-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q11590885}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Kandani Shrine in Kagawa Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]
Honden (National Treasure) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|19|29.47|N|133|55|00.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kandani Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}}) |本殿 = Three-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Kandani Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:神谷神社 正面鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Front torii]] '''Kandani Shrine''' (かんだにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === It stands on the slope of Shiramine-yama, one of the peaks of {{ill|lt=Goshikidai|Q11372616|WD=Q11372616|1=Goshikidai}}, in the eastern part of Sakaide. It is located in a valley called "Kandani," with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kandani River|1=UNKNOWN}} flowing north of the precinct. Its honden is the oldest known {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} shrine building with a clearly known construction year[http://www.shikoku.gr.jp/spot/380 Kandani Shrine], and is designated a National Treasure. Although it possesses a National Treasure shrine building, the precinct itself is relatively small. It also preserves shrine treasures including nationally designated Important Cultural Property standing attendant statues. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deities''' * '''{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}''' (ほむすびのみこと) * '''Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひこのみこと) * '''Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-kami|1=Futsunushi}} (ふつぬしのかみ) ** {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} (たけみかづちのかみ) ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (あめのこやねのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=Hime Ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} (ひめおおかみ) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the valley where the shrine stands has long been called "Kandani." Behind the shrine is a giant rock called "Eikō-seki," identified as an ancient {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred rock, and Yayoi pottery has been found nearby. Regarding its founding, tradition states that in {{ill|lt=812|Q31114|WD=Q31114|1=812}} ({{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3), Atto Ōtaru, uncle of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, built shrine buildings and invited the Kasuga Four Deities into the co-enshrined hall. In {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7), it was granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper; in {{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}} (Jogan 10), Senior Fifth Rank Lower. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}} ({{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5), it appears in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} as "Kandani Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. The ridge beam of the current honden bears an ink inscription from {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} ({{ill|lt=Kenpo|Q1194251|WD=Q1194251|1=Kempo (era)}} 7): "Treasure hall of Senior First Rank Kandani Daimyojin... begun on 10th day, 2nd month, year of tsuchinoto-u... by manor official Gyōbu no Sukune Masanaga," showing it had reached the highest divine rank and regional reverence at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system. On New Year's Eve of {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110|1=1927}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 2), a lamp fire ignited the haiden, causing a fire. The haiden burned down, but residents pulled the collapsing building away from the honden side, saving it. The present haiden was rebuilt from {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} (Showa 3) to {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Showa 5). On {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|WD=Q2884|1=September 27}}, {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), lightning struck the honden and about one-third of the roof was burned{{Cite web|和書|title=Kagawa: roof of National Treasure honden burned / lightning strike at Kandani Shrine |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/national/social/20220927000464 |date=2022-09-27 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2022-09-27}}. On October 12, {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|WD=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), a ground-breaking ceremony for preservation and repair was held, with completion planned for autumn 2025{{Cite web|和書|title=First step toward restoration of National Treasure honden: ground-breaking at Kandani Shrine in Sakaide, roof repair planned to finish autumn 2025 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/dg/article.aspx?id=K2023101300000001400 |date=2023-10-13 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2023-10-15}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), October 9: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kandani deity". * Jogan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), May 27: Senior Fifth Rank Lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Kandani Tenjin". === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri shrine building rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|WD=Q5388|1=1219}} (Kenpo 7), early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, by manor official Gyōbu Masanaga from the old honden. It is the oldest known shrine architecture in Japan with a clear construction date and is designated a National Treasure. About 50 meters behind the shrine buildings stands a giant rock called "Eikō-seki." It is said that in ancient times worship was performed there as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred site. ファイル:神谷神社 拝所.JPG|Haisho ファイル:神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii ファイル:神谷神社 御旅所.JPG|Otabisho
Located beside the first torii. === Sessha and massha === === Sessha === [[ファイル:Kandani-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Aragami Shrine]] * Aragami Shrine - enshrined partway along the approach. === Massha === Many are enshrined within the precinct. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Honden - designated {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|WD=Q2313|1=February 2}}, {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} (Showa 30). === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Two wooden standing attendant statues *: A pair of A and Un figures, considered early Kamakura works. Designated {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}}, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|WD=Q2649|1=1966}} (Showa 41). === Sakaide City designated tangible cultural properties === * Jinja Hōraku Renga (calligraphy) *: A 100-verse {{ill|lt=renga|Q1506883|WD=Q1506883|1=Renga}} sequence performed before the shrine in {{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|WD=Q6687|1=1493}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2), written on four half-folded torinoko sheets. Designated Feb 21, 1958. * Two bugaku masks (craftworks) *: Two masks for Batō and Kangenraku. Designated Nov 3, 1961. * Hand-copied Daihannya Haramita Sutra, 600 volumes (documents) *: Inscribed {{ill|lt=1455|Q6480|WD=Q6480|1=1455}} ({{ill|lt=Kyotoku|Q1143370|WD=Q1143370|1=Kyōtoku}} 4) and {{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 13). Designated Jan 10, 1967. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Sakaide Station|Q873215|WD=Q873215|1=Sakaide Station}} ({{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Kotohira Bus|Q11573750|WD=Q11573750|1=Kotosan Bus}} (bound for Ogoshi), get off at "Takaya Minami" (about 15 minutes), then walk 20 minutes. === References === * ''Engishiki-listed National Treasure Kandani Shrine'' (shrine history booklet) * Kagawa Historical Walk Editorial Committee, ''New Edition: Historical Walks in Kagawa Prefecture'', Yamakawa Publishing, 1996, p.28 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]

Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=812|Q31114|qid=Q31114|1=812}}) |本殿 = Three-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Kandani Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:神谷神社 正面鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Front torii]] '''Kandani Shrine''' (かんだにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|qid=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === It stands on the slope of Shiramine-yama, one of the peaks of {{ill|lt=Goshikidai|Q11372616|qid=Q11372616|1=Goshikidai}}, in the eastern part of Sakaide. It is located in a valley called "Kandani," with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kandani River|1=UNKNOWN}} flowing north of the precinct. Its honden is the oldest known {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} shrine building with a clearly known construction year[http://www.shikoku.gr.jp/spot/380 Kandani Shrine], and is designated a National Treasure. Although it possesses a National Treasure shrine building, the precinct itself is relatively small. It also preserves shrine treasures including nationally designated Important Cultural Property standing attendant statues. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deities''' * '''{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}''' (ほむすびのみこと) * '''Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひこのみこと) * '''Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-kami|1=Futsunushi}} (ふつぬしのかみ) ** {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} (たけみかづちのかみ) ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (あめのこやねのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=Hime Ōkami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} (ひめおおかみ) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the valley where the shrine stands has long been called "Kandani." Behind the shrine is a giant rock called "Eikō-seki," identified as an ancient {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred rock, and Yayoi pottery has been found nearby. Regarding its founding, tradition states that in {{ill|lt=812|Q31114|qid=Q31114|1=812}} ({{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3), Atto Ōtaru, uncle of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, built shrine buildings and invited the Kasuga Four Deities into the co-enshrined hall. In {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|qid=Q30350|1=865}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7), it was granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper; in {{ill|lt=868|Q25061|qid=Q25061|1=868}} (Jogan 10), Senior Fifth Rank Lower. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|qid=Q24239|1=905}} ({{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5), it appears in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|qid=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} as "Kandani Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. The ridge beam of the current honden bears an ink inscription from {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|qid=Q5388|1=1219}} ({{ill|lt=Kenpo|Q1194251|qid=Q1194251|1=Kempo (era)}} 7): "Treasure hall of Senior First Rank Kandani Daimyojin... begun on 10th day, 2nd month, year of tsuchinoto-u... by manor official Gyōbu no Sukune Masanaga," showing it had reached the highest divine rank and regional reverence at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system. On New Year's Eve of {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|qid=Q18110|1=1927}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 2), a lamp fire ignited the haiden, causing a fire. The haiden burned down, but residents pulled the collapsing building away from the honden side, saving it. The present haiden was rebuilt from {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|qid=Q19715|1=1928}} (Showa 3) to {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|qid=Q18787|1=1930}} (Showa 5). On {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|qid=Q2884|1=September 27}}, {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|qid=Q49625|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), lightning struck the honden and about one-third of the roof was burned{{Cite web|和書|title=Kagawa: roof of National Treasure honden burned / lightning strike at Kandani Shrine |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/national/social/20220927000464 |date=2022-09-27 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2022-09-27}}. On October 12, {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|qid=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), a ground-breaking ceremony for preservation and repair was held, with completion planned for autumn 2025{{Cite web|和書|title=First step toward restoration of National Treasure honden: ground-breaking at Kandani Shrine in Sakaide, roof repair planned to finish autumn 2025 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/dg/article.aspx?id=K2023101300000001400 |date=2023-10-13 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2023-10-15}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|qid=Q30350|1=865}}), October 9: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kandani deity". * Jogan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|qid=Q23816|1=875}}), May 27: Senior Fifth Rank Lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Kandani Tenjin". === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri shrine building rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|qid=Q5388|1=1219}} (Kenpo 7), early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, by manor official Gyōbu Masanaga from the old honden. It is the oldest known shrine architecture in Japan with a clear construction date and is designated a National Treasure. About 50 meters behind the shrine buildings stands a giant rock called "Eikō-seki." It is said that in ancient times worship was performed there as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred site. ファイル:神谷神社 拝所.JPG|Haisho ファイル:神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii ファイル:神谷神社 御旅所.JPG|Otabisho
Located beside the first torii. === Sessha and massha === === Sessha === [[ファイル:Kandani-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Aragami Shrine]] * Aragami Shrine - enshrined partway along the approach. === Massha === Many are enshrined within the precinct. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Honden - designated {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|qid=Q2313|1=February 2}}, {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|qid=Q18577|1=1955}} (Showa 30). === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Two wooden standing attendant statues *: A pair of A and Un figures, considered early Kamakura works. Designated {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|qid=Q2617|1=June 11}}, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|qid=Q2649|1=1966}} (Showa 41). === Sakaide City designated tangible cultural properties === * Jinja Hōraku Renga (calligraphy) *: A 100-verse {{ill|lt=renga|Q1506883|qid=Q1506883|1=Renga}} sequence performed before the shrine in {{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|qid=Q6687|1=1493}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2), written on four half-folded torinoko sheets. Designated Feb 21, 1958. * Two bugaku masks (craftworks) *: Two masks for Batō and Kangenraku. Designated Nov 3, 1961. * Hand-copied Daihannya Haramita Sutra, 600 volumes (documents) *: Inscribed {{ill|lt=1455|Q6480|qid=Q6480|1=1455}} ({{ill|lt=Kyotoku|Q1143370|qid=Q1143370|1=Kyōtoku}} 4) and {{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|qid=Q6631|1=1481}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 13). Designated Jan 10, 1967. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|qid=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Sakaide Station|Q873215|qid=Q873215|1=Sakaide Station}} ({{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|qid=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|qid=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Kotohira Bus|Q11573750|qid=Q11573750|1=Kotosan Bus}} (bound for Ogoshi), get off at "Takaya Minami" (about 15 minutes), then walk 20 minutes. === References === * ''Engishiki-listed National Treasure Kandani Shrine'' (shrine history booklet) * Kagawa Historical Walk Editorial Committee, ''New Edition: Historical Walks in Kagawa Prefecture'', Yamakawa Publishing, 1996, p.28 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kandani Shrine entry === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Kandani-jinja}} * 神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG {{commons category|Category:Kandani-jinja}} [[EN:Kandani Shrine]] [[JA:神谷神社]] [[SV:Kandani-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q11590885}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Kandani Shrine in Kagawa Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]
Honden (National Treasure) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|qid=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|19|29.47|N|133|55|00.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kandani Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=812|Q31114|qid=Q31114|1=812}}) |本殿 = Three-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Kandani Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:神谷神社 正面鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Front torii]] '''Kandani Shrine''' (かんだにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|qid=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === It stands on the slope of Shiramine-yama, one of the peaks of {{ill|lt=Goshikidai|Q11372616|qid=Q11372616|1=Goshikidai}}, in the eastern part of Sakaide. It is located in a valley called "Kandani," with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kandani River|1=UNKNOWN}} flowing north of the precinct. Its honden is the oldest known {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} shrine building with a clearly known construction year[http://www.shikoku.gr.jp/spot/380 Kandani Shrine], and is designated a National Treasure. Although it possesses a National Treasure shrine building, the precinct itself is relatively small. It also preserves shrine treasures including nationally designated Important Cultural Property standing attendant statues. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deities''' * '''{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}''' (ほむすびのみこと) * '''Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひこのみこと) * '''Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (おくつひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-kami|1=Futsunushi}} (ふつぬしのかみ) ** {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} (たけみかづちのかみ) ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (あめのこやねのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=Hime Ōkami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} (ひめおおかみ) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the valley where the shrine stands has long been called "Kandani." Behind the shrine is a giant rock called "Eikō-seki," identified as an ancient {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred rock, and Yayoi pottery has been found nearby. Regarding its founding, tradition states that in {{ill|lt=812|Q31114|qid=Q31114|1=812}} ({{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 3), Atto Ōtaru, uncle of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, built shrine buildings and invited the Kasuga Four Deities into the co-enshrined hall. In {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|qid=Q30350|1=865}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7), it was granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper; in {{ill|lt=868|Q25061|qid=Q25061|1=868}} (Jogan 10), Senior Fifth Rank Lower. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|qid=Q24239|1=905}} ({{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5), it appears in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|qid=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} as "Kandani Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. The ridge beam of the current honden bears an ink inscription from {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|qid=Q5388|1=1219}} ({{ill|lt=Kenpo|Q1194251|qid=Q1194251|1=Kempo (era)}} 7): "Treasure hall of Senior First Rank Kandani Daimyojin... begun on 10th day, 2nd month, year of tsuchinoto-u... by manor official Gyōbu no Sukune Masanaga," showing it had reached the highest divine rank and regional reverence at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system. On New Year's Eve of {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|qid=Q18110|1=1927}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 2), a lamp fire ignited the haiden, causing a fire. The haiden burned down, but residents pulled the collapsing building away from the honden side, saving it. The present haiden was rebuilt from {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|qid=Q19715|1=1928}} (Showa 3) to {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|qid=Q18787|1=1930}} (Showa 5). On {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|qid=Q2884|1=September 27}}, {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|qid=Q49625|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), lightning struck the honden and about one-third of the roof was burned{{Cite web|和書|title=Kagawa: roof of National Treasure honden burned / lightning strike at Kandani Shrine |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/national/social/20220927000464 |date=2022-09-27 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2022-09-27}}. On October 12, {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|qid=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), a ground-breaking ceremony for preservation and repair was held, with completion planned for autumn 2025{{Cite web|和書|title=First step toward restoration of National Treasure honden: ground-breaking at Kandani Shrine in Sakaide, roof repair planned to finish autumn 2025 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/dg/article.aspx?id=K2023101300000001400 |date=2023-10-13 |publisher=Shikoku Shimbun |accessdate=2023-10-15}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|qid=Q30350|1=865}}), October 9: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kandani deity". * Jogan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|qid=Q23816|1=875}}), May 27: Senior Fifth Rank Lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Kandani Tenjin". === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri shrine building rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1219|Q5388|qid=Q5388|1=1219}} (Kenpo 7), early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, by manor official Gyōbu Masanaga from the old honden. It is the oldest known shrine architecture in Japan with a clear construction date and is designated a National Treasure. About 50 meters behind the shrine buildings stands a giant rock called "Eikō-seki." It is said that in ancient times worship was performed there as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} sacred site. ファイル:神谷神社 拝所.JPG|Haisho ファイル:神谷神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii ファイル:神谷神社 御旅所.JPG|Otabisho
Located beside the first torii. === Sessha and massha === === Sessha === [[ファイル:Kandani-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Aragami Shrine]] * Aragami Shrine - enshrined partway along the approach. === Massha === Many are enshrined within the precinct. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Honden - designated {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|qid=Q2313|1=February 2}}, {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|qid=Q18577|1=1955}} (Showa 30). === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Two wooden standing attendant statues *: A pair of A and Un figures, considered early Kamakura works. Designated {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|qid=Q2617|1=June 11}}, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|qid=Q2649|1=1966}} (Showa 41). === Sakaide City designated tangible cultural properties === * Jinja Hōraku Renga (calligraphy) *: A 100-verse {{ill|lt=renga|Q1506883|qid=Q1506883|1=Renga}} sequence performed before the shrine in {{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|qid=Q6687|1=1493}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2), written on four half-folded torinoko sheets. Designated Feb 21, 1958. * Two bugaku masks (craftworks) *: Two masks for Batō and Kangenraku. Designated Nov 3, 1961. * Hand-copied Daihannya Haramita Sutra, 600 volumes (documents) *: Inscribed {{ill|lt=1455|Q6480|qid=Q6480|1=1455}} ({{ill|lt=Kyotoku|Q1143370|qid=Q1143370|1=Kyōtoku}} 4) and {{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|qid=Q6631|1=1481}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 13). Designated Jan 10, 1967. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|qid=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kandani-cho 621 '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Sakaide Station|Q873215|qid=Q873215|1=Sakaide Station}} ({{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|qid=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|qid=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Kotohira Bus|Q11573750|qid=Q11573750|1=Kotosan Bus}} (bound for Ogoshi), get off at "Takaya Minami" (about 15 minutes), then walk 20 minutes. === References === * ''Engishiki-listed National Treasure Kandani Shrine'' (shrine history booklet) * Kagawa Historical Walk Editorial Committee, ''New Edition: Historical Walks in Kagawa Prefecture'', Yamakawa Publishing, 1996, p.28 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kandani Shrine entry === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Kandani-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkashinkou/kandanijinjya.html National Treasure Kandani Shrine Honden] (Sakaide City website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated Shrine|ja|神谷神社|sv|Kandani-jinja}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Kandani Shrine in Kagawa Prefecture|Kandani Shrine in Kyotango, Kyoto|Kandani Tachi-no-miya Shrine}} {{神社 |名称 = Kandani Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kandani Shrine.JPG|280px|Honden (National Treasure)]]
